items.title: Natural Gas Power Burns Rebound in Latest Storage Week
items.summary: Natural gas demand strengthened during the latest storage week as heating loads returned to more typical late-winter levels, setting up a larger pull in Thursdayís U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) report.

items.title: Futures Settle: War in Iran Intensifies and Lower 48 Natural Gas Prices Pop
items.summary: Natural gas futures rallied beyond the $3.00/MMBtu level on Tuesday, bolstered by threats to global supply amid the potential for a long and wide war in the Middle East.

items.title: MidDay Snapshot: Weak Weather Sinks Northeast Natural Gas Prices as Waha, AECO Climb
items.summary: Day-ahead spot natural gas pricing was mixed through Tuesday afternoon, with stout gains in Texas, Canada and the Midwest offset by a second straight day of steep declines in the Northeast.

items.title: LNG Markets Face Extreme Volatility as Middle East War Intensifies, Supply Disruptions Indefinite
items.summary: An intensifying war in the Middle East wreaked havoc in energy markets Tuesday, with natural gas bearing the worst of it, but analysts predicted supply disruptions could be short-lived despite American and Israeli claims that their attacks against Ir

items.title: Judge Blocks Shellís Effort to Revisit Venture Global LNG Contract Ruling
items.summary: Shell plcís gambit to overturn an arbitration decision in Venture Global Inc.ís favor has ended after the New York Supreme Court refused to hear its petition to vacate.

items.title: MidDay Futures: Cooler Forecast Trends, Iran War Boosting Natural Gas Prices
items.summary: War-induced disruptions to Middle East LNG supply, plus incremental heating demand in domestic two-week weather forecasts, drove intraday gains for April natural gas futures on Tuesday.

items.title: Midmorning Markets: Natural Gas Futures Hold Onto Gains as Middle East Risks Mount
items.summary: Natural gas futures jumped Tuesday morning as escalating Middle East tensions stoked fears of prolonged disruptions to global LNG supply, with U.S. prices riding the tailwinds of overseas benchmarks higher.

items.title: Cameron LNG Working to Restore Production After Brief Outage Amid Global Supply Shock
items.summary: Operations were ramping back up at Cameron LNG in Louisiana on Tuesday after the plant was briefly shut down on Monday afternoon.

items.title: Is Fertilizer Supply at Risk? CF Industries Cites Challenges for NatGas Products Amid War
items.summary: Executives at CF Industries Holdings Inc. said high European natural gas feedstock costs favored North American production of ammonia and ammonia-derived nitrogen products for use as fertilizer.

items.title: Market Open: Nymex Natural Gas Jumps on Colder Forecasts, Middle East LNG Risks
items.summary: U.S. natural gas futures climbed early Tuesday as colder weather forecasts and an escalating Middle East conflict buoyed sentiment across global gas markets.

items.title: Sanctioned Russian LNG Tanker Explodes in Mediterranean ó A First of Its Kind?
items.summary: A Russian-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) tanker under U.S. and UK sanctions caught fire in the Mediterranean early Tuesday following a reported explosion, with the fate of its crew initially unclear, according to Reuters and the Times of Malta.

items.title: Canadaís Heavy Crude Tightens as Hormuz Risk Ripples Through Global Markets
items.summary: Canadian heavy crude rallied Tuesday as geopolitical risk in the Middle East began reshuffling global barrels in real time. Heavy Western Canadian Select strengthened in Alberta to a discount of $11.80 per barrel to the monthly average of West Texas

items.title: UK Gas Prices Have Nearly Doubled This Week
items.summary: Gas prices in the UK have nearly doubled as the war between US and Iran ramped up since the weekend. The price of UK wholesale gas prices ñ which reflect the costs energy suppliers pay to producers for natural gas before selling to household and busi

items.title: Hormuz Freeze Sends Brent-Dubai Spread to Multi-Year High
items.summary: Brentís premium to the Middle Eastís Dubai benchmark has blown out to its widest level since 2022, confirmation that the global oil market is squarely trading on disruption. As of Tuesday morning, Brent was trading around $83ñ$84 per barrel, up more

items.title: How U.S. Control of Venezuela Threatens Ecuadorís Broken Oil Industry
items.summary: U.S. intervention in Venezuela removed a major destabilizing force in South Americaís Northern Andes region, the authoritarian regime of illegitimate president Nicolas Maduro. While this will revitalize Venezuelaís economic backbone, the countryís oi
